text: 13.9g of fat. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet. in this category, carbs barely matter.
18.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 300% daily value, vitamin d at 90% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
minerals are basically not adding much here.

on the plus side: easy to centre a meal around. practical meal value matters. gives a straightforward protein solution. the category often wins on simplicity and reliability. complete protein delivery is reliable. the category is supposed to do this well.

on the weaker side: the outcome depends heavily on preparation. a good raw profile can still be cooked into a worse result. fat quality can go sideways fast. some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. cost and storage can be a pain. practical downsides still count.
A tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
